# loop-compound-capture

Captures what a completed work cycle taught and routes the learning to the
tier where it compounds. Use this skill at merge, after a PR lands, at
engagement closeout, and whenever a cycle surfaced something that changed how
the work was done: a debugging insight, a convention worth keeping, a
procedure performed for the second time, a client-domain fact that will
matter again. If a PR just merged and this skill has not run, run it.

Without this loop the pipeline is linear and every cycle's lessons evaporate
with the context window. This loop is the write-back edge: it is what makes
the system compound instead of merely execute.

## Trigger

- A PR merges.
- An engagement closes out.
=== canonical pin semantics ===
52:	//   - URL       — a remote git repository (cloned + pinned by Commit).
53:	//   - LocalPath — a local git repository / clone (opened + pinned by Commit).
55:	//                 filesystem. Unpinned by nature: it has no git object store,
69:	// Commit is the pinned git commit SHA (40 hex). Pinning is the default for
92:	// Version is a free-form version pin for the adapter. Compared by

trusted_sha: 9cee577c8e96a8c859d37def58ab41584755c6da

  - codex
  - cursor
  - pi

content.text:
Both items are now grounded — and item 1 is two real defects, not just missing content:

- The emitted `~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` files have **no YAML frontmatter at all**, so Claude Code falls back to the first line as the description — which is the injected managed-header comment. That's exactly what appeared in your skills list this session.
- That header itself ships **uninterpolated placeholders**: `Source: {source-url}@{short-sha}` — the template variables are never filled in.

And for item 2: today the canonical source is pinned by commit SHA + `trusted_sha` (TOFU) in the manifest, so picking up upstream changes means hand-editing two hashes and re-running sync. There is no update flow at all — `watch` only watches the local filesystem, and the repo's AGENTS.md forbids background daemons, which constrains the design.

Here's the revised scope I'm proposing to plan against:

Two workstreams in one plan. **Skill usability**: every synced skill surfaces a real description to the target tool — fix the uninterpolated `{source-url}@{short-sha}` header placeholders, emit proper frontmatter on Claude-bound skills, and surface canonical skills that lack a description instead of silently emitting garbage. **Upstream freshness**: a first-class way to pick up new commits from the pinned GitHub source and re-sync through the existing materialize/trust/atomic-swap machinery — no hand-editing hashes, and no background daemon (that constraint is load-bearing in AGENTS.md).

**Call outs:**
- Missing-description handling: coverage-style warning vs. hard decode error vs. auto-deriving a description from the skill's first heading/paragraph (warning matches the tool's existing posture; auto-derive risks wrong text in the tool's UI).
- Update trigger shape: an explicit `agent-sync update` command vs. a check-and-repin folded into every `sync` vs. remote polling inside the existing `watch` loop (explicit command is the most TOFU-friendly; on-sync is the most "it just works").
- Trust on update: re-pinning to a new upstream commit updates `trusted_sha` — silently (trust the channel) vs. an interactive confirm showing old→new SHA (preserves the supply-chain stance in the threat model).

Confirm and I'll proceed to research, drawing on this scope. (If you'd rather split these into two plans, say so — the description fix is small and could ship while the update flow is still being designed.)

content: Planning agent-sync's next features: skill-description fixes (synced skills currently show garbage descriptions) and auto-updating from the pinned GitHub source. Waiting on your confirmation of the proposed scope and call-outs before starting research. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text: Confirmed. Dispatching Phase 1 research — repo analysis and institutional learnings in parallel:

input.description: Research skill emission and update paths
input.prompt:
Scope: technology, architecture, patterns in /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync (Go CLI, read AGENTS.md first).

Planning context: agent-sync syncs canonical agent config (skills, rules, commands, agents-md, MCP entries, plugins) from a git-pinned canonical repo into tool-specific outputs via bundled adapters (claude, cursor, codex, pi), through an IR decode → adapter wire protocol → atomic-swap engine with per-target ledgers and TOFU trust (manifest carries canonical.url + commit + trusted_sha). We are planning two workstreams:

(W1) Skill descriptions: the claude adapter emits `.claude/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md` with NO YAML frontmatter, so Claude Code falls back to the first line — the injected managed-header HTML comment — as the skill description. Worse, that header ships uninterpolated template placeholders: `<!-- Managed by agent-sync — do not edit. Source: {source-url}@{short-sha}. Regenerate: agent-sync sync -->`. We need to know exactly where these defects live.

(W2) Upstream update flow: today updating means hand-editing `commit:` and `trusted_sha:` in .agent-sync.yaml and re-running sync. We want a first-class flow to fetch new upstream commits and re-pin + re-sync through existing machinery. No background daemons (AGENTS.md constraint).

Research and report (with repo-relative file paths and key line references):

1. Skill emission per adapter: where claude/codex/pi adapters build SKILL.md file ops; where the managed header is injected for skills; where `{source-url}` / `{short-sha}` template placeholders are defined and why they're not interpolated (what data the adapter receives — does the wire payload carry source URL + commit SHA? check irwire / adapter protocol initialize params and Node.Provenance); whether codex/pi skills share the same header path (they must stay byte-identical for ADV-1 co-ownership — check how the shared .agents/skills emission keeps claude/codex/pi copies in sync).
2. IR skill decode: how skills/<id>/SKILL.md is decoded (internal/ir/decode.go), what frontmatter is parsed/stripped (extractFrontmatter), what fields ir.Node carries that could hold a description, and whether the canonical SKILL.md's own frontmatter (if present) survives to the emitted output or is stripped.
3. Warning infrastructure: internal/coverage warning shape + how warnings surface in sync output; decode warnings (ir.Warning) and how they reach the user. Which is the right seam for a "skill missing description" warning.
4. Manifest read/write: manifest.LoadFile and any existing manifest WRITE path (does `init` write the manifest? cmd_init.go, resolvepin — how is the initial commit+trusted_sha resolved?). Strict schema handling for adding fields. Is there precedent for the CLI editing .agent-sync.yaml in place?
5. Trust/materialize path: internal/trust TOFU store semantics, internal/git fetch/clone/cache (internal/cache), how sync materializes the pinned commit, what happens on trusted_sha mismatch (exit codes 3/4/5?), offline-strict mode, non-interactive gating of trust prompts.
6. CLI command registration pattern (internal/cli/root.go + cmd_*.go) and the hooks command (--post-merge git hook) — precedent for event-driven re-sync. watch command structure (what it polls, its loop, whether a remote-poll could slot in).
7. Test conventions for these areas (hierarchy_sync_test.go harness, adapter contract/conformance tests, gittest helpers).

Return a structured report keyed by the 7 areas, dense with file:line references. Note any surprises or constraints that would materially shape the plan.

Planning context: agent-sync (Go CLI) — we're planning (1) fixing skill emission so Claude-bound skills carry frontmatter descriptions and the managed-header template placeholders ({source-url}@{short-sha}) actually interpolate, plus a decode/coverage warning for skills missing descriptions; (2) an upstream-update flow that fetches new commits from the pinned canonical git repo and re-pins commit+trusted_sha through the TOFU trust gate — no background daemons.

Relevant learning topics: adapter emission conventions (byte-identical shared-subdir outputs, ADV-1 co-ownership), managed markers/headers, trust/TOFU decisions and their rationale, manifest schema strictness, capability-lie gate (adapters must not claim kinds they emit nothing for), warning-surfacing conventions, any prior attempts or decisions about auto-update/pin refresh, watch command lessons, non-interactive mode constraints.

Return: each applicable learning with its file path, the lesson in one or two sentences, and how it constrains or informs this plan. If nothing applies, say so plainly.

content.text:
## Institutional Learnings Search Results

### Search Context
- **Feature/Task**: Planning (1) skill-emission fix — frontmatter descriptions on Claude-bound skills, managed-header `{source-url}@{short-sha}` placeholder interpolation, decode/coverage warning for description-less skills; (2) upstream-update flow re-pinning commit+trusted_sha through the TOFU trust gate.
- **Keywords Used**: tofu, trusted_sha, trust gate, managed header/marker, frontmatter, description, skill, capability-lie, byte-identical, co-own, warning, watch, pin, non-interactive, placeholder/interpolate
- **Files Scanned**: 3 solution docs + 3 handoffs (all grep-matched; all frontmatter/full-read as needed)
- **Relevant Matches**: 6 (2 solutions strong, 1 moderate; all 3 handoffs contain applicable learnings)

Note: `docs/solutions/patterns/critical-patterns.md` does not exist in this repo.

### Relevant Learnings

#### 1. Capability-lie gate — warnings must not be an adapter's only output for a "supported" kind
- **File**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md` (Learnings section; also `/Users/justinbeaudry/.claude/projects/-Users-justinbeaudry-Projects-agent-sync/memory/capability-lie-gate-scope.md`)
- **Module**: `internal/adapter/runtime.go`, `internal/coverage`
- **Problem Type**: `logic_error` / convention (inferred — handoff, no frontmatter)
- **Relevance**: Directly constrains the "warn on skills missing descriptions" feature. The runtime fails any session where a `supported` kind has an in-target IR node but the adapter emits **zero non-warning ops**. If a description-less skill causes the adapter to emit only a warning and skip the write, that trips the gate (this bit the Cursor adapter in PR #31).
- **Key Insight**: Design the warning as warning-plus-emit (emit the skill anyway, degraded) or route it through `internal/coverage` at the decode layer, not as an adapter-level skip. If skipping is intended, the kind must be demoted to `unsupported` for that scope.
- **Severity**: high

#### 2. Marker/header ownership contract — engine owns managed markers, adapters send inner body only
- **File**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/handoffs/2026-06-10-001-codex-adapter-shared-subdir-handoff.md` (commits `1a1899f`/`20c00c7`) and `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/handoffs/2026-06-08-001-mvp-progress-handoff.md` (item 4, marker-ownership; "No wall-clock in cores")
- **Module**: `internal/merge`, `internal/engine`, bundled adapters
- **Problem Type**: `architecture_pattern` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: Almost certainly the root-cause shape of the `{source-url}@{short-sha}` non-interpolation bug. There was already a latent bug (in both codex and cursor) where adapters wrapped content in markers that the engine owns; the merge engine rejects bodies containing manager markers. Separately, cores take **caller-stamped** timestamps/SHAs (`Meta`, `generatedAt`) — the engine stamps once; adapters/templates never compute them.
- **Key Insight**: When fixing placeholder interpolation, first establish which layer owns the managed header and where `Meta` (source URL, SHA) is stamped — the fix belongs at the engine-stamping seam, not inside adapter templates. Adapters must keep sending inner body only.
- **Severity**: high

#### 3. Load-bearing on-disk identifiers — managed markers, frontmatter keys, and emitted skill dirs are wire-format contracts
- **File**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/solutions/best-practices/large-mechanical-rename-of-load-bearing-identifiers.md`
- **Module**: repo-wide
- **Problem Type**: `best_practice`
- **Relevance**: The skill-emission fix touches exactly the class-3 identifiers this doc names: markdown managed-block markers, IR frontmatter keys, emitted skill/rules dirs, sidecar markers. Changing the managed-header template or frontmatter shape changes bytes that ledgers hash and drift-detection compares.
- **Key Insight**: Treat header/marker/frontmatter strings as producer/consumer contracts, not cosmetics — enumerate every consumer (ledger hashing, drift scan, merge marker parser, tests) before changing emitted bytes, and verify with exhaustive grep plus the schema-parity tests.
- **Severity**: high

#### 4. Shared-subdir skill emission is data-loss-critical and co-owned (ADV-1)
- **File**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/handoffs/2026-06-10-001-codex-adapter-shared-subdir-handoff.md` (shared-subdir fix, per-leaf sentinel P1) and `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md` (PRs #33/#34)
- **Module**: `internal/engine/target.go`, `internal/sync/{staging,swap}`
- **Problem Type**: `architecture_pattern` / data-loss fix (inferred)
- **Relevance**: Skill emission targets `.claude/skills/` and `.agents/skills/` via `OutputModeSharedSubdir`; codex+pi co-own leaves via `ScanDriftUnion`. Changing skill file contents (adding descriptions, fixed headers) is safe, but any change touching emission paths, leaf names, or op shapes interacts with per-leaf sentinels, the effective-prefix set, and union ledgers.
- **Key Insight**: Keep the shared parent untouched, keep emission changes content-only where possible, and run `ce-code-review` before shipping — every prior P1 in this area (shared-subdir orphaning, per-leaf sentinel collision, validate-vs-sync divergence) was caught by adversarial review, not the local gate.
- **Severity**: critical

#### 5. Fail-closed + non-interactive fail-fast constrain the upstream-update flow
- **File**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/handoffs/2026-06-08-001-mvp-progress-handoff.md` (Invariants: "Fail-closed everywhere"; `internal/cli/nonint.go`)
- **Module**: `internal/engine`, `internal/cli`
- **Problem Type**: `convention` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: The update flow (fetch → verify → re-pin commit+trusted_sha) mutates the manifest/trust store. Repo convention: on any error, write nothing / leave on-disk byte-identical; durable state (ledger) is written before destructive steps. And `--non-interactive` propagates fail-fast context — a TOFU re-pin prompt must fail fast rather than hang or silently accept under `--non-interactive`.
- **Key Insight**: Structure the update as fetch/verify fully → prompt (or fail-fast non-interactively) → atomic re-pin write; never a partially updated pin. No prior auto-update/pin-refresh attempt exists in solutions or handoffs — this is greenfield, but the trust rationale to honor lives in `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/spec/trust-store-v1.md` and `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/threat-model.md` (specs, not learnings — read before designing the gate).
- **Severity**: high

#### 6. Reconcile spec-vs-impl drift deliberately; cross-platform path code needs build tags (moderate, adjacent)
- **Files**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/solutions/workflow-issues/spec-impl-drift-at-pr-review-2026-04-25.md`; `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/solutions/best-practices/go-windows-cross-platform-pitfalls-2026-04-24.md`
- **Module**: `internal/ir`; `internal/fsroot`/`internal/cache`
- **Problem Type**: `workflow_issue`; `best_practice`
- **Relevance**: (a) The skill/description contract is spec'd in `docs/spec/` (IR v1, manifest v1) — when your fix diverges from the spec, decide which side is authoritative and update both in one commit rather than fast-applying a reviewer patch (drift here surfaced before, on the IR decoder itself). (b) The upstream-update flow will do git fetch + cache/path work; any new syscall- or path-comparison code needs `_unix.go`/`_windows.go` build tags and slash-normalized comparisons — `t.Skip` at runtime does not protect against Windows compile failures.
- **Severity**: medium / high

### Recommendations
- Locate the `Meta`-stamping seam in the engine before touching templates — the placeholder bug is likely "adapter template expects values the engine never passes," mirroring the marker-ownership bug fixed in PR #24. Fix at the stamping layer; keep adapters emitting inner bodies.
- For the missing-description warning, decide skip-vs-degrade explicitly and check it against the capability-lie gate in `internal/adapter/runtime.go`; surface via `internal/coverage` like the Cursor user-scope precedent (#31).
- Treat both work items as data-loss-adjacent: TDD, then `ce-code-review` before PR (it caught every P1 in this exact area), and update `docs/plans/` first if the design diverges.
- For the update flow: read `docs/spec/trust-store-v1.md` + `docs/threat-model.md` for the original TOFU rationale, honor fail-closed atomic re-pin, wire `--non-interactive` fail-fast, and — per the repo's Do-not list and the plan itself — no background daemons; there are no watch-command learnings because no watch command was ever built.
- Nothing in the knowledge base covers upstream/pin-refresh directly; after this lands, capture the TOFU re-pin design with `/ce-compound`.

- **Problem Type**: `workflow_issue`; `best_practice`
- **Relevance**: (a) The skill/description contract is spec'd in `docs/spec/` (IR v1, manifest v1) — when your fix diverges from the spec, decide which side is authoritative and update both in one commit rather than fast-applying a reviewer patch (drift here surfaced before, on the IR decoder itself). (b) The upstream-update flow will do git fetch + cache/path work; any new syscall- or path-comparison code needs `_unix.go`/`_windows.go` build tags and slash-normalized comparisons — `t.Skip` at runtime does not protect against Windows compile failures.
- **Severity**: medium / high

### Recommendations
- Locate the `Meta`-stamping seam in the engine before touching templates — the placeholder bug is likely "adapter template expects values the engine never passes," mirroring the marker-ownership bug fixed in PR #24. Fix at the stamping layer; keep adapters emitting inner bodies.
- For the missing-description warning, decide skip-vs-degrade explicitly and check it against the capability-lie gate in `internal/adapter/runtime.go`; surface via `internal/coverage` like the Cursor user-scope precedent (#31).
- Treat both work items as data-loss-adjacent: TDD, then `ce-code-review` before PR (it caught every P1 in this exact area), and update `docs/plans/` first if the design diverges.
- For the update flow: read `docs/spec/trust-store-v1.md` + `docs/threat-model.md` for the original TOFU rationale, honor fail-closed atomic re-pin, wire `--non-interactive` fail-fast, and — per the repo's Do-not list and the plan itself — no background daemons; there are no watch-command learnings because no watch command was ever built.
- Nothing in the knowledge base covers upstream/pin-refresh directly; after this lands, capture the TOFU re-pin design with `/ce-compound`.

## Repository Research Summary

Repo root: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync` (all paths below are relative to it).

### Technology & Infrastructure

- **Go 1.25+ single-module CLI** (`go.mod`, cobra + goccy/go-yaml + fsnotify; no CGo). No services, no API surface, no DB. Read `AGENTS.md` first — its 7 invariants are load-bearing: all workspace writes via `internal/fsroot`; adapters emit declarative ops only; non-interactive never prompts; pin-by-default; offline-strict with pinned-cached exception; two-rename atomic swap; ledger-authoritative orphan deletion.
- **Module layout:** `internal/cli` (cobra commands) → `internal/engine` (sync orchestration) → primitives (`ir`, `adapter`, `git`, `cache`, `trust`, `manifest`, `merge`, `ledger`, `sync`, `fsroot`, `locks`, `report`, `coverage`, `hierarchy`, `watch`, `hooks`, `worktree`). Public adapter SDK in `pkg/adapterkit`; wire contract in `internal/adapter/contract`; bundled adapters in `internal/adapter/bundled/{claude,cursor,codex,pi}`.
- **Protocol policy:** "freeze the wire frame, grow capabilities" — new wire fields must be additive/`omitempty` (`AGENTS.md:46-52`, PR checklist `AGENTS.md:99-102`). Both `EmitParams` and `InitializeParams` already carry an unused `Meta json.RawMessage `json:"_meta,omitempty"`` extension point (`internal/adapter/contract/protocol.go:140,193`).

---

### Area 1 — Skill emission per adapter (W1 defect locations)

**Where SKILL.md ops are built:**
- claude: `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_reserved.go:109-159` (`emitSkill`) → `mkdir .claude/skills/agent-sync-<id>` + `write_file .../SKILL.md` with `prependHeader(body)` at line 122. Constants at lines 14-20. Rules/commands share the same header via `prependHeader` (lines 49, 81; helper at 249-255).
- codex: `internal/adapter/bundled/codex/emit_reserved.go:34-84` — emits into **shared** `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>` (constant line 18, "Codex reads .agents/skills, validated June 2026"). `prependHeader` at 130-137.
- pi: `internal/adapter/bundled/pi/emit_reserved.go:34` — comment at lines 25-34 states it is **byte-for-byte identical to codex's emitSkill** (same managed header) so co-emission into `.agents/skills` produces identical bytes.

**The uninterpolated header:** defined per-adapter as `managedHeaderTemplate`:
- `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/header.go:18` — doc comment (lines 8-13) says placeholders "are filled in by the sync engine (Unit 13) once it plumbs IR-decode context through EmitParams._meta. v1 ships the placeholder so the line shape is stable." **That plumbing was never done.**
- `internal/adapter/bundled/codex/header.go:11` and `internal/adapter/bundled/pi/header.go:12` — same string; pi's comment (lines 6-8) explicitly documents the byte-identity requirement with codex. `internal/adapter/bundled/cursor/header.go` has its own copy too (cursor uses MDC frontmatter for rules; check before touching).

**Why not interpolated — the data genuinely isn't on the wire:**
- Wire node shape: `internal/engine/irwire.go:24-44` (`wireNode`/`wireProvenance`) and adapter-side mirror `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit.go:18-38` (`irNode`/`irProvenance`). Provenance carries **only `path` + `blob_sha`** — no source URL, no commit SHA.
- `EmitParams` construction: `internal/adapter/runtime.go:158-161` sets only `Target` + `IR`; `Meta` never populated. `InitializeParams` construction: `internal/adapter/runtime.go:48-56` (client, versions, cookie, workspace_root, reserved_prefix, ir_version, scope) — no source info.
- Session options: `internal/engine/target.go:46-50` (`adapter.SessionOptions{WorkspaceRoot, IRVersion, Scope}`).
- **The engine HAS the commit** (`engine.Request.Commit`, `internal/engine/request.go:79-81`, stamped into report/staging) but **not the canonical URL** — that lives only in the manifest at the CLI layer (`internal/cli/setup.go:123-133` builds the Request from `prepared.Manifest`; URL is dropped). Plumbing path for W1: manifest URL + `mat.Commit` → `engine.Request` → `SessionOptions`/`InitializeParams.Meta` (additive `_meta`, no version bump) → header rendering. Note `local_dir` sources have **no commit** (empty ref → zero-SHA placeholder, `internal/cli/materialize.go:70-83`) — interpolation must handle "no pin" gracefully.

**Byte-identity enforcement (ADV-1 co-ownership):** not enforced by a test on the header itself — it's an engine invariant. `internal/engine/target.go:280-321` documents "INVARIANT (identical co-emission)": cross-adapter shared-leaf release assumes co-owners emit byte-identical SKILL.md, with a fail-closed guard (line 309-315) that errors on divergent co-emission. **Constraint for W1: any header interpolation must produce identical bytes from codex and pi for the same node** (same source URL + SHA inputs — true by construction since both come from the one manifest, but keep the rendering helper shared or byte-tested). Tests to update: `internal/adapter/bundled/{claude,codex,cursor}/header_test.go` each assert the literal `{source-url}@{short-sha}` placeholder must remain (e.g. `codex/header_test.go:18-20`); emit tests only assert prefix `"<!-- Managed by agent-sync"` (`claude/emit_test.go:91,152,176`; `codex/emit_test.go:71`; `pi/emit_test.go:69`; `engine/shared_subdir_test.go:513`) and survive interpolation. Conformance corpus (`internal/adapter/conformance/corpus/happy-skill.json`) checks `{op, path}` records only — content-safe.

**W1 defect #2 (missing frontmatter):** the claude adapter writes SKILL.md as `header + stripped body` — there is no code path that emits YAML frontmatter (`name:`/`description:`) for Claude Code skill discovery. See Area 2 for why the body can never contain it either.

### Area 2 — IR skill decode

- `internal/ir/decode.go:111-142` routes `skills/<id>/SKILL.md` → `buildSkillNode` (lines 400-421); assets gathered separately via `SkillsByID` (lines 229-311).
- Frontmatter: `extractFrontmatter` dispatcher in `internal/ir/kinds.go:345-357`; markdown path `extractMarkdownFrontmatter` (lines 62-89) **strips the entire frontmatter block from Body** — the emitted SKILL.md never contains the canonical file's frontmatter.
- **Surprise (materially shapes W1):** the recognized frontmatter schema is exactly `{required, targets, version}` with **strict unknown-field rejection** (`Frontmatter` struct `kinds.go:23-27`; `parseYAMLFrontmatter` `kinds.go:167-187` uses `yaml.DisallowUnknownField()` + `AllowFieldPrefixes("x-")`). A canonical SKILL.md carrying standard Claude `description:`/`name:` frontmatter **fails decode hard** with `ErrUnknownFrontmatterField` (`internal/ir/types.go:152`). So today canonical skills *cannot* even author a description; `x-description:` would parse but be silently discarded (not retained on the struct). Adding a description therefore requires: new `Frontmatter` field + new `ir.Node` field + wire field (`irwire.go` + each adapter's `irNode`) — all additive — plus a spec update (`docs/spec/ir-v1.md`; per `AGENTS.md` the kind set is closed but fields are the engine's to grow; adding a Node field is called out as a v1.x change in `claude/emit_reserved.go:260-262`).
- `ir.Node` today has no description-capable field (`internal/ir/types.go:56-83`): ID, Kind, Version, Required, Targets, Provenance{Path, BlobSHA}, Body.

### Area 3 — Warning infrastructure (right seam for "skill missing description")

Three distinct warning channels:
1. **Decode warnings** (`ir.Warning`, `internal/ir/types.go:120-127`; frozen codes at 164-167: `agents-md-missing`, `skill-asset-unreadable`). Produced by `ir.Decode`/`SkillsByID`, aggregated in `materialized.Warnings` (`internal/cli/materialize.go:29-33,145-157`), surfaced **only as `rc.Logger.Warn` stderr lines** in `prepareScope` (`internal/cli/setup.go:110-112`). Not counted in the report.
2. **Adapter `OpWarning`s** (`internal/adapter/contract/protocol.go:442-451`; adapterkit mirror). Emitted per concept (example: the `paths:` frontmatter warning, `claude/emit_reserved.go:55-61`), exempt from the declared-outputs gate (`runtime.go:181-183`), collected into `emitOutcome.warnings` as `"<conceptID>: <note>"` strings (`internal/engine/target.go:106-108`), **counted** in `report.Counts.Warnings` (`internal/report/summary.go:44`; `engine.go:262`) but the strings only surface in `validate`'s `TargetChange.Warnings` (`internal/engine/plan.go:39,154`) — sync's text render (`report/summary.go:158-172`) doesn't print them. Caveat: the capability-lie gate fails a session where a "supported" kind yields zero non-warning ops (memory note; `runtime.go` gateChecks) — warning-only emission for a kind is dangerous, but adding a warning *alongside* the write ops is safe.
3. **Coverage warnings** (`internal/coverage/coverage.go:28-33`, static (target, kind, level) table) — hierarchy-scope gaps only; wrong seam for this.

**Recommended seam:** a per-node `OpWarning{ConceptID: <skill-id>, Status: degraded}` from each skill-emitting adapter mirrors the existing `paths:` precedent exactly and rides existing plumbing. If you want it once (not 3× per adapter), a decode-time `ir.Warning` with a new frozen code (e.g. `skill-missing-description`) in `buildSkillNode` is the alternative — but it only reaches stderr logs today, and warning codes are spec-frozen (`types.go:163`).

### Area 4 — Manifest read/write (W2 foundation)

- **Strict schema:** `internal/manifest/schema.go` (`Manifest`, `CanonicalSource` with url/local_path/local_dir 1-of-3, `TrustedSHA` mirror at line 26); load via `LoadFile`/`LoadBytes` (`internal/manifest/load.go:89-123`) with `DisallowUnknownField` + `x-` prefix escape. Key invariants in `Validate` (`load.go:125-230`): `trusted_sha` requires `commit`; both 40-hex; **`commit != trusted_sha` is a load error** (line 196-198); non-interactive requires trusted_sha when commit set (line 193).
- **Write path exists and is exactly what W2 needs:** `internal/manifest/write.go:43-90` `WriteResolvedSHA(orig, commit, trustedSHA)` — goccy AST edit preserving comments/key order, updates `$.canonical.commit` + `$.trusted_sha` in one pass; `ErrKeyMissing` if keys absent (init templates pre-declare both keys, `write.go:17-23`). `WriteTrustedSHA` (line 95) for pin-only. `WriteFile` (lines 99-119) does the atomic staged write via fsroot.
- **In-place-edit precedent:** `agent-sync trust pin --sha` and `trust promote --pin-manifest` (`internal/cli/cmd_trust.go:505-540`, `writePinToManifest` at 527) already read/patch/rewrite `.agent-sync.yaml`, with a URL-match safety gate (`assertPromotedURLMatchesManifest`, lines 474-501) and a post-write reload check pattern in init (`cmd_init.go:117-122`).
- **init/resolvepin:** `internal/cli/cmd_init.go:146-188` — `resolvePin` resolves ref→SHA (local via `git.ResolveLocalRef`, remote via `cache.Canonicalize` + `git.ResolveRef`; refuses under `--offline`), then the wizard's `ManifestYAML()` renders commit + trusted_sha together. This is the resolve-then-write shape a `sync --update`/`update` command would reuse.
- Adding a new manifest field: add to `schema.go` with `omitempty` — strict load means old binaries reject new keys unless `x-` prefixed; consider that for compatibility.

### Area 5 — Trust / materialize path

- **Flow:** `internal/cli/materialize.go:57-139` — `local_dir` skips git/trust/offline entirely (74-83); `local_path` requires pin (`ErrFloatingLocalUnsupported`, line 22); `url` → `cache.Canonicalize` → `cache.Resolve` (`internal/cache/location.go`, key from `internal/cache/key.go`) → `git.Materialize`.
- **git.Materialize** (`internal/git/materialize.go:92-215`): pinned+offline+cached fast path (124-137, `ErrUnresolvablePinOffline` otherwise); online path bare-clone-via-scratch (226-258) or `Fetch` (153), ref resolution for floating/defer-resolve (159-173), **force-push reachability defense** `ErrReachabilityCheckFailed` when both pin and ref set (188-200). `git.ResolveRef` (`internal/git/resolve.go:25`) is the network ls-remote resolver.
- **Trust gate:** `trust.Decide` pure policy (`internal/trust/policy.go:90-265`); exit codes 3/4/5 = `ExitRevokedTrustAnchor`/`ExitTrustDecisionRequired`/`ExitFirstUseDenied` (`internal/trust/types.go:31-33`), mapped via `ExitCodeFor` (policy.go:61-74) and `exitError` → `MapExit` (`internal/cli/exit.go`).
- **Big surprise for W2:** sync calls `Decide` with **TTY unset and no trust/pending stores wired** (`materialize.go:122-131`; `_ = dec` at 131 discards `AppendPending`/`Reminder`). Consequences: (a) pin mismatch always fails closed exit 4 even interactively; (b) **`trust pending` is never populated by sync**, so `trust promote --pin-manifest`'s documented flow has no feeder; (c) more fundamentally, for a pinned manifest `git.Materialize` returns `ResolvedSHA == pin` — **sync never even learns a newer upstream SHA exists**. A first-class update flow must itself do: `Fetch(cacheDir)` → `ResolveRef(url, ref)` (network) → compare to pin → `WriteResolvedSHA` (both keys, keeping the commit==trusted_sha invariant) → re-run existing sync machinery. Non-interactive gating precedent: `requireFlag` (`internal/cli/nonint.go`), `--accept-new-source=<sha>` / `=any` + peer gate `AGENT_SYNC_ALLOW_UNSAFE_ANY=1` (policy.go:11-22, 174-197).
- TOFU store: append-only JSONL fold (`internal/trust/store.go`, `pending.go`), typed-confirmation prompts (`prompt.go`) used only by `trust add/revoke/reset`.

### Area 6 — CLI command registration, hooks, watch

- **Pattern:** `newXCommand(deps RootDeps) *cobra.Command` factories mounted in `NewRootCommand` (`internal/cli/root.go:141-158`); per-invocation `runtimeContext` (Access/Logger/Flags) injected via `PersistentPreRunE` context (root.go:115-125), retrieved with `mustRuntime`. Persistent flags: `--workspace --output --log-level --offline --non-interactive/--yes` (root.go:132-138). Dep bundles with test-injectable streams/clock (`RootDeps`, `TrustDeps`).
- **Hooks (event-driven re-sync precedent):** `internal/cli/cmd_hooks.go` — explicit `--install-hooks` opt-in, never prompts; `internal/hooks/install.go` writes marker-tagged (`# agent-sync-managed-hook v1`) POSIX wrappers for `post-merge` + `post-checkout` running `exec <abs-path> sync --post-merge --workspace <ws>` (install.go:166,193), with foreign-hook `--replace/--append` handling and lefthook/husky advisories (line 207). `sync --post-merge` caps the run-lock wait at 3s and yields exit 0 writing `.agent-sync/state/hook-skipped` (`internal/cli/cmd_sync.go:19-25,99-107,155-160,206-211`) so `git pull` never breaks.
- **Watch:** `internal/cli/cmd_watch.go` + `internal/watch/watch.go` — foreground **fsnotify** (not poll) debounced loop (default 500ms), watches manifest + local source paths only (cmd_watch.go:47-59), self-loop guard via `IgnorePrefixes`, SIGINT/SIGTERM cancel, failure marker `last-watch.failed` for `status`. **It never watches the remote** — a remote-poll would be a new timer-driven case in `watch.Run`'s select or a separate ticker path; note `AGENTS.md` "no background daemons" is honored precisely because watch is foreground-with-context ("deliberately not a daemon", watch.go:3-7) — a W2 poller can follow the same shape.
- No `update`/`unmanage` command exists yet (root.go mounts init, sync, status, validate, hooks, watch, trust, adapter) — despite adapter READMEs advertising `agent-sync unmanage claude` (`claude/header.go:33,75`). W2's command would be a new `cmd_*.go` factory.

### Area 7 — Test conventions

- **Style:** table-driven std `testing` (no testify anywhere), `t.TempDir`, real filesystem under fsroot per `AGENTS.md:82-84`. Gate: `go test -race ./...` + `golangci-lint run` (CLAUDE.md overlay). Known CI quirk: darwin/amd64 runner stalls are infra, not code.
- **gittest:** `internal/gittest/gittest.go` — `RequireGit(t)` (skip, or hard-fail under `AGENT_SYNC_REQUIRE_GIT` in CI) and `MustGit(t, dir, args...)` with a hermetic env (pinned identity, `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, no global config). Use for any W2 fetch/resolve tests.
- **hierarchy_sync_test.go harness** (`internal/cli/hierarchy_sync_test.go:24-60+`): builds home/repo/nested trees with `local_dir: .agents` manifests (no git/network/pin), `writeWS` helper, `newTestRuntime` for a fake runtimeContext — the go-to harness for end-to-end CLI sync assertions without a remote.
- **Adapter emit tests:** fixture-driven via `testdata/ir/*.json` through the production `handleEmit` path (`claude/emit_test.go:17-50` `emitFixture`/`captureOps`), asserting op records + decoded content prefixes. Header tests per adapter (`*/header_test.go`) — the placeholder assertions there must flip in W1.
- **Conformance:** `internal/adapter/conformance/` harness runs a subprocess adapter binary against a JSON corpus (`corpus/*.json`, 16 cases; `spec_locked_test.go` freezes the corpus) checking `{op, path}` expectations only. `conformance/echo` is the reference adapter. Engine-level shared-subdir/co-ownership behavior tested in `internal/engine/shared_subdir_test.go`; manifest write round-trips in `internal/manifest/write_test.go`; init pin resolution in `internal/cli/cmd_init_resolvepin_test.go`.

### Key surprises / constraints for the plan

1. **W1 is three defects, not one:** (a) header placeholders never interpolated (data absent from wire; `EmitParams._meta` is the pre-reserved additive slot); (b) no frontmatter emitted on SKILL.md at all; (c) canonical SKILL.md **cannot legally author** `description:` today — strict frontmatter rejects it at decode (`ir/kinds.go:175`). Fixing (c) means touching Frontmatter, ir.Node, the wire node in `engine/irwire.go` **and** each adapter's local `irNode` mirror, plus `docs/spec/ir-v1.md`.
2. **codex/pi SKILL.md bytes must stay identical** after any header change (engine fail-closed guard, `engine/target.go:294-321`) — and claude's header should probably render identically too for consistency, though claude's tree isn't shared.
3. **W2 has no existing "newer SHA" detection**: pinned sync never resolves the ref; the pending/promote review flow exists but is unfed. The clean W2 shape is a new command reusing `git.Fetch` + `git.ResolveRef` + reachability check + `manifest.WriteResolvedSHA` + the existing sync entry (`prepareEngine`/`runHierarchySync`), with `trust.Decide`-style non-interactive gating (exit 4 + exact-flag remediation) and optionally finally wiring pending-entry appends.
4. Protocol/adapter changes must be additive (`omitempty`, backward-compat test) or bump the contract version (`AGENTS.md:99-102`); plan divergence requires updating `docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md` first in the same commit.

Scope: technology, architecture, patterns in /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync (Go CLI, read AGENTS.md first).

Planning context: agent-sync syncs canonical agent config (skills, rules, commands, agents-md, MCP entries, plugins) from a git-pinned canonical repo into tool-specific outputs via bundled adapters (claude, cursor, codex, pi), through an IR decode → adapter wire protocol → atomic-swap engine with per-target ledgers and TOFU trust (manifest carries canonical.url + commit + trusted_sha). We are planning two workstreams:

(W1) Skill descriptions: the claude adapter emits `.claude/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md` with NO YAML frontmatter, so Claude Code falls back to the first line — the injected managed-header HTML comment — as the skill description. Worse, that header ships uninterpolated template placeholders: `<!-- Managed by agent-sync — do not edit. Source: {source-url}@{short-sha}. Regenerate: agent-sync sync -->`. We need to know exactly where these defects live.

(W2) Upstream update flow: today updating means hand-editing `commit:` and `trusted_sha:` in .agent-sync.yaml and re-running sync. We want a first-class flow to fetch new upstream commits and re-pin + re-sync through existing machinery. No background daemons (AGENTS.md constraint).

Research and report (with repo-relative file paths and key line references):

1. Skill emission per adapter: where claude/codex/pi adapters build SKILL.md file ops; where the managed header is injected for skills; where `{source-url}` / `{short-sha}` template placeholders are defined and why they're not interpolated (what data the adapter receives — does the wire payload carry source URL + commit SHA? check irwire / adapter protocol initialize params and Node.Provenance); whether codex/pi skills share the same header path (they must stay byte-identical for ADV-1 co-ownership — check how the shared .agents/skills emission keeps claude/codex/pi copies in sync).
2. IR skill decode: how skills/<id>/SKILL.md is decoded (internal/ir/decode.go), what frontmatter is parsed/stripped (extractFrontmatter), what fields ir.Node carries that could hold a description, and whether the canonical SKILL.md's own frontmatter (if present) survives to the emitted output or is stripped.
3. Warning infrastructure: internal/coverage warning shape + how warnings surface in sync output; decode warnings (ir.Warning) and how they reach the user. Which is the right seam for a "skill missing description" warning.
4. Manifest read/write: manifest.LoadFile and any existing manifest WRITE path (does `init` write the manifest? cmd_init.go, resolvepin — how is the initial commit+trusted_sha resolved?). Strict schema handling for adding fields. Is there precedent for the CLI editing .agent-sync.yaml in place?
5. Trust/materialize path: internal/trust TOFU store semantics, internal/git fetch/clone/cache (internal/cache), how sync materializes the pinned commit, what happens on trusted_sha mismatch (exit codes 3/4/5?), offline-strict mode, non-interactive gating of trust prompts.
6. CLI command registration pattern (internal/cli/root.go + cmd_*.go) and the hooks command (--post-merge git hook) — precedent for event-driven re-sync. watch command structure (what it polls, its loop, whether a remote-poll could slot in).
7. Test conventions for these areas (hierarchy_sync_test.go harness, adapter contract/conformance tests, gittest helpers).

Return a structured report keyed by the 7 areas, dense with file:line references. Note any surprises or constraints that would materially shape the plan.

- `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/header.go:18` — doc comment (lines 8-13) says placeholders "are filled in by the sync engine (Unit 13) once it plumbs IR-decode context through EmitParams._meta. v1 ships the placeholder so the line shape is stable." **That plumbing was never done.**
- `internal/adapter/bundled/codex/header.go:11` and `internal/adapter/bundled/pi/header.go:12` — same string; pi's comment (lines 6-8) explicitly documents the byte-identity requirement with codex. `internal/adapter/bundled/cursor/header.go` has its own copy too (cursor uses MDC frontmatter for rules; check before touching).

**Why not interpolated — the data genuinely isn't on the wire:**
- Wire node shape: `internal/engine/irwire.go:24-44` (`wireNode`/`wireProvenance`) and adapter-side mirror `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit.go:18-38` (`irNode`/`irProvenance`). Provenance carries **only `path` + `blob_sha`** — no source URL, no commit SHA.
- `EmitParams` construction: `internal/adapter/runtime.go:158-161` sets only `Target` + `IR`; `Meta` never populated. `InitializeParams` construction: `internal/adapter/runtime.go:48-56` (client, versions, cookie, workspace_root, reserved_prefix, ir_version, scope) — no source info.
- Session options: `internal/engine/target.go:46-50` (`adapter.SessionOptions{WorkspaceRoot, IRVersion, Scope}`).
- **The engine HAS the commit** (`engine.Request.Commit`, `internal/engine/request.go:79-81`, stamped into report/staging) but **not the canonical URL** — that lives only in the manifest at the CLI layer (`internal/cli/setup.go:123-133` builds the Request from `prepared.Manifest`; URL is dropped). Plumbing path for W1: manifest URL + `mat.Commit` → `engine.Request` → `SessionOptions`/`InitializeParams.Meta` (additive `_meta`, no version bump) → header rendering. Note `local_dir` sources have **no commit** (empty ref → zero-SHA placeholder, `internal/cli/materialize.go:70-83`) — interpolation must handle "no pin" gracefully.

**Byte-identity enforcement (ADV-1 co-ownership):** not enforced by a test on the header itself — it's an engine invariant. `internal/engine/target.go:280-321` documents "INVARIANT (identical co-emission)": cross-adapter shared-leaf release assumes co-owners emit byte-identical SKILL.md, with a fail-closed guard (line 309-315) that errors on divergent co-emission. **Constraint for W1: any header interpolation must produce identical bytes from codex and pi for the same node** (same source URL + SHA inputs — true by construction since both come from the one manifest, but keep the rendering helper shared or byte-tested). Tests to update: `internal/adapter/bundled/{claude,codex,cursor}/header_test.go` each assert the literal `{source-url}@{short-sha}` placeholder must remain (e.g. `codex/header_test.go:18-20`); emit tests only assert prefix `"<!-- Managed by agent-sync"` (`claude/emit_test.go:91,152,176`; `codex/emit_test.go:71`; `pi/emit_test.go:69`; `engine/shared_subdir_test.go:513`) and survive interpolation. Conformance corpus (`internal/adapter/conformance/corpus/happy-skill.json`) checks `{op, path}` records only — content-safe.

**W1 defect #2 (missing frontmatter):** the claude adapter writes SKILL.md as `header + stripped body` — there is no code path that emits YAML frontmatter (`name:`/`description:`) for Claude Code skill discovery. See Area 2 for why the body can never contain it either.

### Area 2 — IR skill decode

- `internal/ir/decode.go:111-142` routes `skills/<id>/SKILL.md` → `buildSkillNode` (lines 400-421); assets gathered separately via `SkillsByID` (lines 229-311).
- Frontmatter: `extractFrontmatter` dispatcher in `internal/ir/kinds.go:345-357`; markdown path `extractMarkdownFrontmatter` (lines 62-89) **strips the entire frontmatter block from Body** — the emitted SKILL.md never contains the canonical file's frontmatter.
- **Surprise (materially shapes W1):** the recognized frontmatter schema is exactly `{required, targets, version}` with **strict unknown-field rejection** (`Frontmatter` struct `kinds.go:23-27`; `parseYAMLFrontmatter` `kinds.go:167-187` uses `yaml.DisallowUnknownField()` + `AllowFieldPrefixes("x-")`). A canonical SKILL.md carrying standard Claude `description:`/`name:` frontmatter **fails decode hard** with `ErrUnknownFrontmatterField` (`internal/ir/types.go:152`). So today canonical skills *cannot* even author a description; `x-description:` would parse but be silently discarded (not retained on the struct). Adding a description therefore requires: new `Frontmatter` field + new `ir.Node` field + wire field (`irwire.go` + each adapter's `irNode`) — all additive — plus a spec update (`docs/spec/ir-v1.md`; per `AGENTS.md` the kind set is closed but fields are the engine's to grow; adding a Node field is called out as a v1.x change in `claude/emit_reserved.go:260-262`).
- `ir.Node` today has no description-capable field (`internal/ir/types.go:56-83`): ID, Kind, Version, Required, Targets, Provenance{Path, BlobSHA}, Body.

### Area 3 — Warning infrastructure (right seam for "skill missing description")

Three distinct warning channels:
1. **Decode warnings** (`ir.Warning`, `internal/ir/types.go:120-127`; frozen codes at 164-167: `agents-md-missing`, `skill-asset-unreadable`). Produced by `ir.Decode`/`SkillsByID`, aggregated in `materialized.Warnings` (`internal/cli/materialize.go:29-33,145-157`), surfaced **only as `rc.Logger.Warn` stderr lines** in `prepareScope` (`internal/cli/setup.go:110-112`). Not counted in the report.
2. **Adapter `OpWarning`s** (`internal/adapter/contract/protocol.go:442-451`; adapterkit mirror). Emitted per concept (example: the `paths:` frontmatter warning, `claude/emit_reserved.go:55-61`), exempt from the declared-outputs gate (`runtime.go:181-183`), collected into `emitOutcome.warnings` as `"<conceptID>: <note>"` strings (`internal/engine/target.go:106-108`), **counted** in `report.Counts.Warnings` (`internal/report/summary.go:44`; `engine.go:262`) but the strings only surface in `validate`'s `TargetChange.Warnings` (`internal/engine/plan.go:39,154`) — sync's text render (`report/summary.go:158-172`) doesn't print them. Caveat: the capability-lie gate fails a session where a "supported" kind yields zero non-warning ops (memory note; `runtime.go` gateChecks) — warning-only emission for a kind is dangerous, but adding a warning *alongside* the write ops is safe.
3. **Coverage warnings** (`internal/coverage/coverage.go:28-33`, static (target, kind, level) table) — hierarchy-scope gaps only; wrong seam for this.

**Recommended seam:** a per-node `OpWarning{ConceptID: <skill-id>, Status: degraded}` from each skill-emitting adapter mirrors the existing `paths:` precedent exactly and rides existing plumbing. If you want it once (not 3× per adapter), a decode-time `ir.Warning` with a new frozen code (e.g. `skill-missing-description`) in `buildSkillNode` is the alternative — but it only reaches stderr logs today, and warning codes are spec-frozen (`types.go:163`).

### Area 4 — Manifest read/write (W2 foundation)

- **Strict schema:** `internal/manifest/schema.go` (`Manifest`, `CanonicalSource` with url/local_path/local_dir 1-of-3, `TrustedSHA` mirror at line 26); load via `LoadFile`/`LoadBytes` (`internal/manifest/load.go:89-123`) with `DisallowUnknownField` + `x-` prefix escape. Key invariants in `Validate` (`load.go:125-230`): `trusted_sha` requires `commit`; both 40-hex; **`commit != trusted_sha` is a load error** (line 196-198); non-interactive requires trusted_sha when commit set (line 193).
- **Write path exists and is exactly what W2 needs:** `internal/manifest/write.go:43-90` `WriteResolvedSHA(orig, commit, trustedSHA)` — goccy AST edit preserving comments/key order, updates `$.canonical.commit` + `$.trusted_sha` in one pass; `ErrKeyMissing` if keys absent (init templates pre-declare both keys, `write.go:17-23`). `WriteTrustedSHA` (line 95) for pin-only. `WriteFile` (lines 99-119) does the atomic staged write via fsroot.
- **In-place-edit precedent:** `agent-sync trust pin --sha` and `trust promote --pin-manifest` (`internal/cli/cmd_trust.go:505-540`, `writePinToManifest` at 527) already read/patch/rewrite `.agent-sync.yaml`, with a URL-match safety gate (`assertPromotedURLMatchesManifest`, lines 474-501) and a post-write reload check pattern in init (`cmd_init.go:117-122`).
- **init/resolvepin:** `internal/cli/cmd_init.go:146-188` — `resolvePin` resolves ref→SHA (local via `git.ResolveLocalRef`, remote via `cache.Canonicalize` + `git.ResolveRef`; refuses under `--offline`), then the wizard's `ManifestYAML()` renders commit + trusted_sha together. This is the resolve-then-write shape a `sync --update`/`update` command would reuse.
- Adding a new manifest field: add to `schema.go` with `omitempty` — strict load means old binaries reject new keys unless `x-` prefixed; consider that for compatibility.

### Area 5 — Trust / materialize path

- **Flow:** `internal/cli/materialize.go:57-139` — `local_dir` skips git/trust/offline entirely (74-83); `local_path` requires pin (`ErrFloatingLocalUnsupported`, line 22); `url` → `cache.Canonicalize` → `cache.Resolve` (`internal/cache/location.go`, key from `internal/cache/key.go`) → `git.Materialize`.
- **git.Materialize** (`internal/git/materialize.go:92-215`): pinned+offline+cached fast path (124-137, `ErrUnresolvablePinOffline` otherwise); online path bare-clone-via-scratch (226-258) or `Fetch` (153), ref resolution for floating/defer-resolve (159-173), **force-push reachability defense** `ErrReachabilityCheckFailed` when both pin and ref set (188-200). `git.ResolveRef` (`internal/git/resolve.go:25`) is the network ls-remote resolver.
- **Trust gate:** `trust.Decide` pure policy (`internal/trust/policy.go:90-265`); exit codes 3/4/5 = `ExitRevokedTrustAnchor`/`ExitTrustDecisionRequired`/`ExitFirstUseDenied` (`internal/trust/types.go:31-33`), mapped via `ExitCodeFor` (policy.go:61-74) and `exitError` → `MapExit` (`internal/cli/exit.go`).
- **Big surprise for W2:** sync calls `Decide` with **TTY unset and no trust/pending stores wired** (`materialize.go:122-131`; `_ = dec` at 131 discards `AppendPending`/`Reminder`). Consequences: (a) pin mismatch always fails closed exit 4 even interactively; (b) **`trust pending` is never populated by sync**, so `trust promote --pin-manifest`'s documented flow has no feeder; (c) more fundamentally, for a pinned manifest `git.Materialize` returns `ResolvedSHA == pin` — **sync never even learns a newer upstream SHA exists**. A first-class update flow must itself do: `Fetch(cacheDir)` → `ResolveRef(url, ref)` (network) → compare to pin → `WriteResolvedSHA` (both keys, keeping the commit==trusted_sha invariant) → re-run existing sync machinery. Non-interactive gating precedent: `requireFlag` (`internal/cli/nonint.go`), `--accept-new-source=<sha>` / `=any` + peer gate `AGENT_SYNC_ALLOW_UNSAFE_ANY=1` (policy.go:11-22, 174-197).
- TOFU store: append-only JSONL fold (`internal/trust/store.go`, `pending.go`), typed-confirmation prompts (`prompt.go`) used only by `trust add/revoke/reset`.

### Area 6 — CLI command registration, hooks, watch

- **Pattern:** `newXCommand(deps RootDeps) *cobra.Command` factories mounted in `NewRootCommand` (`internal/cli/root.go:141-158`); per-invocation `runtimeContext` (Access/Logger/Flags) injected via `PersistentPreRunE` context (root.go:115-125), retrieved with `mustRuntime`. Persistent flags: `--workspace --output --log-level --offline --non-interactive/--yes` (root.go:132-138). Dep bundles with test-injectable streams/clock (`RootDeps`, `TrustDeps`).
- **Hooks (event-driven re-sync precedent):** `internal/cli/cmd_hooks.go` — explicit `--install-hooks` opt-in, never prompts; `internal/hooks/install.go` writes marker-tagged (`# agent-sync-managed-hook v1`) POSIX wrappers for `post-merge` + `post-checkout` running `exec <abs-path> sync --post-merge --workspace <ws>` (install.go:166,193), with foreign-hook `--replace/--append` handling and lefthook/husky advisories (line 207). `sync --post-merge` caps the run-lock wait at 3s and yields exit 0 writing `.agent-sync/state/hook-skipped` (`internal/cli/cmd_sync.go:19-25,99-107,155-160,206-211`) so `git pull` never breaks.
- **Watch:** `internal/cli/cmd_watch.go` + `internal/watch/watch.go` — foreground **fsnotify** (not poll) debounced loop (default 500ms), watches manifest + local source paths only (cmd_watch.go:47-59), self-loop guard via `IgnorePrefixes`, SIGINT/SIGTERM cancel, failure marker `last-watch.failed` for `status`. **It never watches the remote** — a remote-poll would be a new timer-driven case in `watch.Run`'s select or a separate ticker path; note `AGENTS.md` "no background daemons" is honored precisely because watch is foreground-with-context ("deliberately not a daemon", watch.go:3-7) — a W2 poller can follow the same shape.
- No `update`/`unmanage` command exists yet (root.go mounts init, sync, status, validate, hooks, watch, trust, adapter) — despite adapter READMEs advertising `agent-sync unmanage claude` (`claude/header.go:33,75`). W2's command would be a new `cmd_*.go` factory.

### Area 7 — Test conventions

- **Style:** table-driven std `testing` (no testify anywhere), `t.TempDir`, real filesystem under fsroot per `AGENTS.md:82-84`. Gate: `go test -race ./...` + `golangci-lint run` (CLAUDE.md overlay). Known CI quirk: darwin/amd64 runner stalls are infra, not code.
- **gittest:** `internal/gittest/gittest.go` — `RequireGit(t)` (skip, or hard-fail under `AGENT_SYNC_REQUIRE_GIT` in CI) and `MustGit(t, dir, args...)` with a hermetic env (pinned identity, `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, no global config). Use for any W2 fetch/resolve tests.
- **hierarchy_sync_test.go harness** (`internal/cli/hierarchy_sync_test.go:24-60+`): builds home/repo/nested trees with `local_dir: .agents` manifests (no git/network/pin), `writeWS` helper, `newTestRuntime` for a fake runtimeContext — the go-to harness for end-to-end CLI sync assertions without a remote.
- **Adapter emit tests:** fixture-driven via `testdata/ir/*.json` through the production `handleEmit` path (`claude/emit_test.go:17-50` `emitFixture`/`captureOps`), asserting op records + decoded content prefixes. Header tests per adapter (`*/header_test.go`) — the placeholder assertions there must flip in W1.
- **Conformance:** `internal/adapter/conformance/` harness runs a subprocess adapter binary against a JSON corpus (`corpus/*.json`, 16 cases; `spec_locked_test.go` freezes the corpus) checking `{op, path}` expectations only. `conformance/echo` is the reference adapter. Engine-level shared-subdir/co-ownership behavior tested in `internal/engine/shared_subdir_test.go`; manifest write round-trips in `internal/manifest/write_test.go`; init pin resolution in `internal/cli/cmd_init_resolvepin_test.go`.

### Key surprises / constraints for the plan

1. **W1 is three defects, not one:** (a) header placeholders never interpolated (data absent from wire; `EmitParams._meta` is the pre-reserved additive slot); (b) no frontmatter emitted on SKILL.md at all; (c) canonical SKILL.md **cannot legally author** `description:` today — strict frontmatter rejects it at decode (`ir/kinds.go:175`). Fixing (c) means touching Frontmatter, ir.Node, the wire node in `engine/irwire.go` **and** each adapter's local `irNode` mirror, plus `docs/spec/ir-v1.md`.
2. **codex/pi SKILL.md bytes must stay identical** after any header change (engine fail-closed guard, `engine/target.go:294-321`) — and claude's header should probably render identically too for consistency, though claude's tree isn't shared.
3. **W2 has no existing "newer SHA" detection**: pinned sync never resolves the ref; the pending/promote review flow exists but is unfed. The clean W2 shape is a new command reusing `git.Fetch` + `git.ResolveRef` + reachability check + `manifest.WriteResolvedSHA` + the existing sync entry (`prepareEngine`/`runHierarchySync`), with `trust.Decide`-style non-interactive gating (exit 4 + exact-flag remediation) and optionally finally wiring pending-entry appends.
4. Protocol/adapter changes must be additive (`omitempty`, backward-compat test) or bump the contract version (`AGENTS.md:99-102`); plan divergence requires updating `docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md` first in the same commit.

1 # Plan Sections 2 3 This reference describes what makes a great implementation plan. It does NOT 4 prescribe how the plan looks on the page — rendering is handled by the 5 format-specific references (`markdown-rendering.md`, `html-rendering.md`). 6 7 ## The outcome 8 9 A great plan enables three audiences to act: 10 11 - **The implementing agent** (`ce-work` or a human) starts from an informed 12 baseline — load-bearing decisions are named, research breadcrumbs orient 13 their own investigation, unit boundaries are clear. The plan gives the 14 implementer a starting point, not a substitute for their own investigation. 15 - **The reviewer** identifies the load-bearing decisions and the boundaries 16 of what's being changed in one pass. 17 - **The future reader** (anyone returning months later) traces why the work 18 was done, what shaped it, and where the artifacts live. 19 20 Sections earn their place by serving one of these audiences. Omit padding. 21 22 ## Decide whether a plan doc is warranted at all 23 24 Not every invocation of `ce-plan` should produce a plan document. For 25 genuinely atomic work, the doc is ceremony — the implementer (whether 26 `ce-work` or a human) can act directly without IDed units, KTDs, or 27 Requirements as a checklist. 28 29 **Bias toward producing a plan.** The risk asymmetry favors writing one: 30 a thin plan doc for small work is mild ceremony, but skipping a plan when 31 one was warranted costs the implementer real time (reinvented decisions, 32 lost unit boundaries, no IDed requirements to verify against). When unsure, 33 write the plan. 34 35 **Skip plan creation only when ALL of these hold:** 36 37 - The work is **atomic** — fits in one commit, no meaningful unit boundaries 38 to break out independently. 39 - There are **no design choices that constrain implementation** — no 40 Key Technical Decisions worth recording. If the work needs the implementer 41 to make a choice between two approaches, those approaches are KTDs and 42 a plan is warranted. 43 - There are **no scope boundaries worth pinning** in writing — the work 44 scope is self-evident from the user's request. 45 - **No upstream artifact** (a brainstorm with R-IDs, an incident report, 46 a deferred-follow-up item from a prior plan) needs traceability through 47 this plan. 48 49 **Stress test the "looks atomic" case.** Many requests look atomic at first 50 glance but hide design decisions: 51 52 - *"Add caching to this endpoint"* — sounds atomic, but TTL, invalidation, 53 cache key shape, and backend selection are all KTDs. Write the plan. 54 - *"Migrate from package A to package B"* — sounds mechanical, but 55 semantic differences between the packages create migration KTDs. Write 56 the plan. 57 - *"Add rate limiting"* — sounds small, but algorithm, scope, and 58 configurability are all KTDs. Write the plan. 59 60 vs. genuine skip cases: 61 62 - *"Fix typo in README line 47"* — atomic, no KTDs, skip the plan. 63 - *"Rename `oldFn` to `newFn` across the repo"* — mechanical, no design 64 choices, skip the plan. 65 - *"Bump dependency X to v2.3.1"* — mechanical, skip the plan (unless the 66 bump introduces breaking changes that warrant unit-by-unit migration). 67 68 When skipping the plan doc, the work proceeds directly to `ce-work` or to 69 implementation, and any decisions made along the way land in the commit 70 message or `docs/solutions/` if they're worth carrying forward. 71 72 ## Hard floor 73 74 When a plan doc is warranted, these sections are present. They carry the 75 contracts downstream consumers depend on. 76 77 - **Summary** — what the plan proposes, in 1-3 lines. Forward-looking; orients 78 the reader before they invest in detail. 79 - **Problem Frame** — why the work is being done. Backward-looking / 80 situational. May merge with Summary for compact plans where the motivation 81 is a single sentence. 82 - **Requirements** (with stable R-IDs) — what must be true after the work 83 ships. Reviewer's checklist; downstream code review verifies against these. 84 - **Key Technical Decisions** (KTDs) — the load-bearing choices that constrain 85 implementation. Each entry is `: `. Without these, the 86 implementer can't tell which design choices are open and which are pinned. 87 - **Implementation Units** (with stable U-IDs) — the discrete units of work, 88 sized so each is independently landable. `ce-work` consumes these to 89 execute. For trivial single-step plans the work may collapse into Summary 90 prose and U-IDs may be omitted; this is rare. 91 92 ## Include when material 93 94 These sections are present when they carry information that isn't covered 95 elsewhere. The test is not "is this a substantial plan?" — it is 96 *"does this specific plan have content this section would surface?"* Filling 97 a section with placeholder prose is worse than omitting it. 98 99 - **High-Level Technical Design** — include when the technical approach has 100 shape that prose alone doesn't carry well: architecture across components, 101 sequencing across processes, state machines, branching gates. 102 Visualizations (component topology, sequence, swim lane, flowchart, 103 data-flow) typically live here. Skip when the approach is a one-paragraph 104 pattern application that the prose itself conveys. 105 106 - **Scope Boundaries** — include when scope is contested, when there are 107 tempting non-goals worth naming explicitly, or when "deferred for later" 108 needs distinguishing from "outside the product's identity." Skip when scope 109 is obvious from Requirements alone. 110 111 - **Open Questions** — include when there are genuinely unresolved items that 112 block planning or implementation. Skip when the plan is complete; an empty 113 "Open Questions: none" section signals false uncertainty. 114 115 - **System-Wide Impact** — include when the change affects cross-cutting 116 concerns (data lifecycles, auth boundaries, performance posture, cardinal 117 rules, shared infrastructure). Skip for changes localized to one component 118 where the impact is self-evident. 119 120 - **Risks & Dependencies** — include when there are real risks worth flagging 121 (external service changes, version pins under churn, behavioral assumptions 122 worth highlighting) or material upstream dependencies. Skip for low-risk 123 localized work. 124 125 - **Acceptance Examples** — include when any requirement has a state-dependent 126 or conditional shape ("When X, Y") where the prose alone leaves ambiguity 127 about edge cases. Skip when all requirements are unconditional and 128 unambiguous. 129 130 - **Documentation / Operational Notes** — include when documentation, 131 monitoring, runbooks, or rollout steps need explicit notes. Skip when the 132 work is purely internal and uses existing operational scaffolding without 133 modification. 134 135 - **Sources / Research** — surface the research that orients the implementer 136 or justifies load-bearing choices. The test: *"if I were the implementer 137 reading this cold, would this breadcrumb help me make better choices?"* 138 Yes → surface (code locations like `services/convex/reports.ts:174-176`, 139 external docs, RFCs, constraints, prior plans — the category is inclusive, 140 not enumerated). Process exhaust (reading the user's prompt, glancing at 141 obvious entry points, restating prose) → omit. Surface inline next to the 142 KTD or unit it justifies, or as a dedicated section — both shapes work. 143 144 ## Agent agency 145 146 The catalog is a floor, not a ceiling. When the plan's content doesn't fit 147 any catalog section, introduce a new one — don't force the content into a 148 section it doesn't belong in. Content drives section choices, not vice 149 versa. 150 151 The agent also picks per artifact: 152 153 - Whether Problem Frame merges into Summary 154 - Sub-groupings (Requirements by capability, KTDs by component, Units phased 155 into milestones) 156 - How much detail each section carries 157 - Whether HTD has one diagram, several, or none — and whether visualizations 158 live in HTD or embedded in other sections 159 160 ## Plan metadata fields 161 162 Every plan carries a small set of stable metadata fields that downstream 163 tooling depends on. The contract is format-independent: in markdown these 164 fields appear as YAML frontmatter at the top of the file; in HTML they 165 appear as visible header text (typically a `
` of `
`/`
` pairs or 166 a stats strip). Field names and semantics are the same across both formats 167 so consumers can locate them without knowing which format produced the 168 plan. 169 170 ### Required 171 172 - **`title`** — verbatim plan title. Matches the H1 (markdown) or document 173 `

` (HTML) so file metadata and visible heading don't drift. 174 - **`type`** — conventional-commit-prefix-aligned classification (`feat`, 175 `fix`, `refactor`, `chore`, `docs`, `perf`, `test`, etc.). Carries the 176 intent the eventual commit message should reflect. 177 - **`status`** — `active` on creation; `ce-work` flips to `completed` on 178 ship. `ce-plan`'s Phase 0.1 resume fast path keys on `active`. In HTML, 179 status MUST render as `{value}` so the flip 180 mechanic can locate and rewrite it by selector (see 181 `references/html-rendering.md`). 182 - **`date`** — creation date in ISO 8601 (`YYYY-MM-DD`), ASCII digits only. 183 184 ### Optional but well-known 185 186 These fields are not required, but when set they have fixed names and 187 semantics so downstream tooling can rely on them: 188 189 - **`origin`** — repo-relative path to an upstream brainstorm requirements 190 doc (e.g., `docs/brainstorms/2026-05-12-pagination-requirements.md`). 191 Set when planning from an upstream brainstorm; carried for traceability 192 and re-resolved when `ce-plan` re-deepens. The HITL Proof flow uses 193 `origin` to trace back to the source brainstorm. 194 - **`deepened`** — ISO 8601 date marking the first time the confidence 195 check substantively strengthened the plan. Presence affects Phase 0.1 196 resume fast-path logic (see `references/deepening-workflow.md`). 197 198 Field names are stable across plan revisions — never rename a field or 199 repurpose its semantics. Agents composing new plans MUST use these exact 200 names; adding new fields is fine, but renaming `status` to `state` or 201 `origin` to `source` breaks the downstream consumers above. 202 203 ## ID and content rules 204 205 These apply regardless of rendering format. 206 207 - **Stable IDs.** R-IDs (Requirements), U-IDs (Implementation Units), A-IDs 208 (if Actors fire), F-IDs (if Flows fire), AE-IDs (if Acceptance Examples 209 fire). IDs are stable across plan revisions — never renumber to "clean 210 up gaps." 211 - **Plain prefix.** `R1.`, `U1.` as bullet prefixes. Do not bold; the prefix 212 is visually distinctive on its own. 213 - **Repo-relative paths.** Always. Never absolute paths in plan content; 214 they break portability across machines, worktrees, teammates. 215 - **No process exhaust.** No "captured at Phase X" notes, no `## Next Steps` 216 pointing to the next skill, no italic provenance lines. Engineering process 217 metadata belongs in commit messages and tool output, not the artifact. 218 - **Group Requirements by concern when they span distinct logical areas.** 219 The trigger is distinct concerns, not item count — even four requirements 220 benefit from grouping if they cover three different topics. Skip grouping 221 only when all requirements are genuinely about the same thing; a long flat 222 list is a smell that subgroups were missed. Group by capability (e.g., 223 "Packaging", "Migration and compatibility", "Contributor workflow"), not by 224 the order requirements were discussed. R-IDs stay continuous across groups 225 (R1, R2 in the first group; R3, R4 in the second; never restart at R1 per 226 group). 227 228 ## Rendering 229 230 The format-specific references describe how to render these sections in each 231 output format: 232 233 - **Markdown rendering:** `references/markdown-rendering.md` 234 - **HTML rendering:** `references/html-rendering.md` 235 236 This reference (`plan-sections.md`) is about WHAT the plan contains; 237 rendering references are about HOW each format presents it. The plan is 238 written in one format — markdown OR HTML, never both — based on the 239 resolved output mode. The section catalog is the same regardless of 240 format. 241
